summary:
Overall Risk Level: High

Key Issue to Fix:
1. SQL Injection Vulnerability – The current implementation directly concatenates user input into the SQL query, which makes it susceptible to SQL injection attacks. This is a critical flaw that must be addressed immediately.

Quick Wins and Hardening Recommendations:
• Implement parameterized queries: Immediately update the affected query to use parameterized queries (prepared statements). This prevents malicious input from altering the intended SQL command structure.
• Input validation: While parameterized queries are the primary defense, implementing robust input validation on user-supplied data serves as an additional layer of security.
• Review similar patterns: Audit related modules or functions within the project to ensure no similar patterns of dynamic query formation exist elsewhere.

Addressing these points will substantially reduce the risk of unauthorized database access, preserving data integrity and improving overall application security.

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.location: query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '{username}'"
findings.description: User input from the request is directly concatenated into the SQL query using an f-string, making it vulnerable to SQL injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries to safely include user input. For example, you can rewrite the query as: conn.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?', (username,)).
